summary: Parallel review: FAIL (2 critical, 14 warning, 9 suggestion). Compound gate: PASS

findingsList.text: - Prior run_1 🟡 blockers resolved: `# SKIP` excluded at line 78; JUnit XML test signatures updated to 3-arg

🟡 `bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:76` — `line.trim()` before `/^not ok\b/` double-counts indented `not ok` lines from TAP subtests; `node --test-reporter tap` (this project's own infra) produces this format; one failing nested test yields `findings.critical = 2` instead of 1 (confirmed empirically by Engineer run_2); resolve the top-level-only vs all-failures design decision before wiring into the gate

🟡 `bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:79` — verbatim attacker-controlled TAP line pushed to `messages[]` without stripping control characters; strip `/[\x00-\x1f\x7f]/g` before push (carried from Security run_1/run_2)

🟡 `bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:76` — `line.trim()` before `/^not ok\b/` double-counts indented `not ok` lines from nested TAP subtests; Node.js test runner (`--test-reporter tap`) produces exactly this format; one failing subtest yields `findings.critical = 2` instead of 1

The stated requirement ("TAP output with one `not ok` line produces one critical finding") is correctly implemented and all 13 tests pass. Prior 🟡 findings from earlier iterations — `# SKIP` not excluded and JUnit XML tests using 1-arg signatures — are both resolved in the current code.
Commit `c23a5bb` applied after the handshake resolved two prior 🟡 issues:
These fix the 🟡 findings raised by Tester, Engineer, Architect, and Security reviewers. Neither changes the core acceptance criterion.

🟡 `.team/features/external-validator-integration/tasks/task-2/handshake.json` — Handshake claims 4 tests; current code has 5 (SKIP test added in `c23a5bb`). Update handshake when a fix commit adds artifacts, so reviewers have accurate evidence.

🟡 `bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:1` — No callers in `bin/`; module is inert in production. Confirm integration task is tracked before marking feature shippable. (Carried from task-1 through all PM reviews.)
No 🔴 critical blockers. No 🟡 warnings (the two prior 🟡 findings from run_1 are resolved).
- Prior 🟡 SKIP exclusion is **resolved** — line 78 now guards `!/# (?:TODO|SKIP)\b/i` with a dedicated test
- Prior 🟡 JUnit XML test call signatures are **resolved** — all 6 call sites updated to 3-arg
- No 🔴 blockers exist; three 🔵 suggestions remain (registry wire-up test, ANSI test coverage, JSDoc accuracy)
The `# SKIP` 🟡 is closed — commit `c23a5bb` extended the guard to `!/# (?:TODO|SKIP)\b/i` (line 78) and added a test at lines 143–151. Verified by direct code read.

🟡 `bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:79` — verbatim attacker-controlled TAP lines pushed to `messages[]` without stripping control characters; `not ok 1 - pwn\x1b[2J` injects ANSI escape sequences into CLI output and log consumers; strip `/[\x00-\x1f\x7f]/g` before `messages.push(trimmed)` (same fix needed at JUnit parser line 49; carried from prior security run)

🟡 `bin/lib/validator-parsers.mjs:84` — non-TAP stdout (stack traces, JSON, empty string) returns `critical: 0` indistinguishable from a clean run; once `getParser("tap")` is wired into the gate, a misconfigured validator silently passes; add `meta.parseWarning = true` when stdout is non-empty and contains no TAP structure (carried from prior security run)
